depression.description: I'm constantly worried about my financial situation. Every time I think about spending money, I feel this overwhelming anxiety that I can't shake off.
I keep declining invitations from friends and family because I can't bear the thought of them knowing how much I'm struggling. It feels safer to isolate myself.
I feel like I can't show my true self to anyone. If I reveal my struggles, I worry that people will see me as weak or a failure.
I can't stop thinking about my failed business. It feels like I've let everyone down, and I can't shake off this feeling of inadequacy.
I feel this immense pressure to meet everyone's expectations, and when I can't, it just reinforces my belief that I'm a failure.

Delusion.topic: Fear of being judged by others
Delusion.description: I constantly worry that if I show any sign of weakness, people will judge me harshly. It's like I have to maintain this facade of control.

Delusion.topic: Belief that discussing problems is pointless
Delusion.description: I often think that talking about my problems won't help and might even make things worse. I feel like nobody wants to hear about my struggles.
I feel like I need to impress others to be accepted. It's exhausting to keep up this performance, but I fear that if I stop, I'll be abandoned.
